rulings.Case Title: DINO BELLUOMINI et al VS. JULIAN ASHLEY BIGGS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Moiton To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 23, 2014, Line 18: DEFENDANT JULIAN BIGGS' Motion To Dismiss The motion is granted. Plaintiffs did not serve the complaint on defendant within three years of filing the action. Plaintiff fails to show that the service at 301 Mission St. on February 28, 2013, was proper. The evidence indicates that the address was not defendant's residence or place of work within the meaning of CCP 415.20. Dismissal of the action is mandatory under CCP 583.210(a) and 583.250. Plaintiffs fail to provide a basis for tolling the 3-year period pursuant to CCP 583.240. Plaintiffs' argument that the time period was tolled because they exercised reasonable diligence fails. In Barrington v. A.H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146, the court explains that the Legislature abrogated that line of authority. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: EDEN GONZALEA HASS et al VS. RHODYCO PRODUCTIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants David Rhody, Individually And Dba Rhodyco And Pamakid Runners Club'S Amended Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 23, 2014, line 19. Defendants David Rhody, And Pamakid Runners Club's Amended Motion For Summary Judgment. Off calendar as to Defendant Pamakid Runners Club's motion for summary judgment per the request of moving party Defendant Pamakid Runners Club. Granted as to RhodyCo Productions' ("Defendant") motion for summary judgment. (CCP § 437c). Defendant has met its initial burden by demonstrating that the Decedent accepted an express waiver and assumption of risk in writing. (Knight v. Jewett (1992) 3 Cal.4th 296, 325 (written words are an express assumption of risk). See UMF no. 8, Defendant's Evidence, Exhibit 4.) In turn, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated that the waiver should be invalidated. First, Defendant was not engaged in the delivery of emergency medical services. Plaintiffs' reliance on Washington case law, Vodopest v. MacGregor (1996) 128 Wash.2d 840, is not dispositive. Plaintiff concedes that there is no direct California authority to support this assertion. (Opp., p. 13:25-28.) Second, in this instance, Plaintiffs are bound by Decedent's agreement to waive Defendant's negligence and assume all risk. (Ruiz v. Podolsky (2010) 50 Cal.4th 838, 851-52.) "The presence or absence of the words 'negligence' or 'bodily injury' is not dispositive. We look instead to the intention of the parties as it appears in the release forms before the court.' " (Sanchez v. Bally's Total Fitness Corp. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 62, 67.) Third, Plaintiffs argue that Defendant engaged in gross negligence. However, gross negligence is not alleged in the Complaint. The pleadings limit the arguments on summary judgment, and the Court does not consider this argument. (Hutton v. Fidelity National Title Company (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 486, 493 (response to a defendant's motion for summary judgment may not create issues outside the pleadings).) Alternatively, there is no dispute that Decedent assumed the primary assumption of risk pertaining to cardiac arrest. (See Opp., p. 2:26-28.) The remaining issue is whether Defendant "increase[d] the risk of injury over that inherent in the activity." (Nalwa v. Cedar Fair, L.P. (2012) 55 Cal.4th 1148, 1154.) Defendant has met it initial burden to demonstrate that it did not increase the risk of injury. In turn, Plaintiffs have not met their shifting burden. The expert evidence proffered by Plaintiffs is not dispositive. (See Towns v. Davidson (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 461, 472-73; American Golf Corp. v. Sup.Ct. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 30, 39.) Further, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated that there are triable issues of fact that Defendant increased the risk of injury. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA DE LA LUZ BELMONTE VS. BAY AREA PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Complainants Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set On And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, April 23, 2014, Line 1, Cross-Complainants Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set On And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. No opposition filed. Sanctions granted and included in the ruling on moving party's Motion to Compel Responses to Demand for Production of Documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS SALAS VS. BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 23, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANTS BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC.'S and BAE SYSTEMS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Part 1 of 2: Granted as to Defendants BAE Systems San Francisco Ship Repair Inc. ("SFSR") and BAE Systems, Inc.'s ("BAE") motion for summary judgment. BAE has met its initial burden of proof to show that no employment relationship existed between Plaintiff and BAE. (Laird v. Capital Cities/ABC, Inc. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 727, 737, disagreed with on another ground in Reid v. Google, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 512, 524. See UMF nos. 9, 42.) In turn, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden to show a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff's belief that he was employed by both Defendants is insufficient to create a triable issue of fact. (Salas Decl., 3.) Notwithstanding whether Plaintiff suffered from a disability, SFSR has met its initial burden of proof to show that Plaintiff's disability was not known to SFSR. "An adverse employment decision cannot be made 'because of' a disability, when the disability is not known to the employer.' [Citations.]" (Avila v. Cont'l Airlines, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1237, 1247.) Although knowledge of the disability can be inferred, "knowledge will only be imputed to the employer when the fact of disability is the only reasonable interpretation of the known facts. 'Vague or conclusory statements revealing an unspecified incapacity are not sufficient . . . .' " (Id. at p. 1248.) Given the circumstances, Plaintiff's statement to SFSR at the random drug test is conclusory. (UMF no. 12.) In turn, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden to demonstrate a triable issue of fact. In the Visit Verification, Dr. Ni did not report that Plaintiff suffered from a disability; Dr. Ni stated the reasons for the visit. (See Salas Decl., Ex. 1.) Dr. Gasparini's deposition does not support Plaintiff's argument. Plaintiff did not discuss any daytime urinary problems. (Plaintiff's Evid., Ex. 5, p. 8:15-19. See Salas Decl., Ex. 3.) Dr. Gasparini reported that in the daytime, Plaintiff stated he voided fairly well. (Id.) Dr. Gasparini's ruled out benign prostatic hypertrophy ("BPH") because "he mentioned that his symptoms were mainly at nighttime. And daytime is - my history says, 'Daytime, patient voids fairly well.' " (Plaintiff's Evid., Ex. 5, p. 13:20-23.) Dr. Gasparini's physical exam indicated that Plaintiff did not suffer from BPH (Id. at p. 13:24 - 14:6, 36:24 - 37:1.) Plaintiff's reliance on Dr. Fan and Dr. Patel's reports does not create a triable issue of fact. Neither doctor reported that Plaintiff suffered from BPH during the adverse employment decisions on Oct. 18 and 24, 2012. Rather, their reports indicate that Plaintiff suffered from BPH during his medical visits from Nov. 4, 2013 onward. (Salas Decl., Exhibits 6, 7.) The accommodation requested by Plaintiff (UMF no. 19) is unreasonable because it would violate the collective bargaining agreement. (McCullah v. So. Calif. Gas Co. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 495, 501. See Defendant's Evid., Ex. 23, Art. 23.2, (initial screening of urine specimen).) Continued to Part 2 of 2 =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CAMAREE ASHBY et al VS. TURNER GRANT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 23, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT TURNER GRANT'S Motion To Strike Complaint. The motion is denied. The motion is untimely because defendant answered the complaint on August 15, 2013. See CCP 435(b)(1). In addition, plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support a claim for exemplary damages. "One who wilfully consumes alcoholic beverages to the point of intoxication, knowing that he thereafter must operate a motor vehicle, thereby combining sharply impaired physical and mental faculties with a vehicle capable of great force and speed, reasonably may be held to exhibit a conscious disregard of the safety of others." Taylor v. Sup. Crt. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 897. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BRENNAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 23, 2014, line 15. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, GEORGE GASCON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Continued to May 30, 2014 to give both parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendant must deliver courtesy copies of the Complaint and Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 23, 2014 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/AJR)
